title: Create shared project structure and protocol contract
description: Create the shared code layout under code/ and define the text protocol contract used by both server and client. Keep this module limited to shared headers and protocol constants so server and client can develop against a stable interface.
acceptance_criteria: WHEN code/include and code/src exist THEN they match the approved directory plan; WHEN protocol names are defined THEN they cover LOGIN, MSG, PRIVATE, LIST, QUIT, OK, ERROR, SYSTEM, CHAT, PRIVATE, USERLIST; ref: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-22-chatroom-design.md:49
review_initial_requirements: Check that shared interfaces are small and do not embed server-only or client-only behavior.
review_regression_requirements: Verify no database, GUI, auth, offline-message, or admin concepts enter the shared contract.
notes: module:common; source_doc:docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-22-chatroom-design.md; picked_reason:common protocol contract unlocks server and client module work; done_at:2026-06-22; skills_used:api-and-interface-design;verification-before-completion; evidence:g++ -std=c++17 -Wall -Wextra -Icode/include -c code/src/protocol.cpp -o /tmp/chatroom_protocol_contract.o exited 0; continue_now:COMMON-02 Implement line framing and protocol parsing

title: Implement line framing and protocol parsing
description: Implement reusable helpers that split newline-delimited TCP input into complete messages and parse commands into validated request objects. Preserve per-connection receive buffers for sticky-packet and partial-packet handling.
acceptance_criteria: WHEN a buffer receives complete and partial newline-delimited messages THEN complete lines are emitted and incomplete data is retained; WHEN commands are parsed THEN invalid forms return structured errors; WHEN a message exceeds 1024 bytes THEN message-too-long is reported; ref: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-22-chatroom-design.md:84
review_initial_requirements: Review parser tests before implementation and confirm each protocol command has success and failure coverage.
review_regression_requirements: Verify nickname rules, 1024-byte message limit, newline framing, sticky-packet handling, and partial-packet buffering all match the approved protocol.

title: Implement reusable server logger
description: Implement a small logger utility for server events and errors, writing to server.log with enough context to support course-paper evidence. Keep logging synchronous and simple for this course design scope.
acceptance_criteria: WHEN logger is called for startup, login, quit, abnormal disconnect, group chat, private chat, and errors THEN server.log receives readable event lines; WHEN logging fails THEN the server can continue with stderr notification; ref: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-22-chatroom-design.md:146
review_initial_requirements: Review the logging API so callers do not duplicate formatting logic.
review_regression_requirements: Verify logged event classes match the approved Logger section and do not include message-history query behavior.

title: Review shared module against approved protocol vision
description: Use a same-model sub-agent review to compare the shared protocol, framing, validation, and logger work against the approved design claims and evidence level.
acceptance_criteria: WHEN all non-review issues before this row are closed THEN run same-model sub-agent review against source-specific claim/evidence checks; WHEN gaps or overstated claims are found THEN append follow-up issues and REVIEW-02; WHEN no gaps remain THEN close the CSV.
review_initial_requirements: Verify all prior non-review rows are closed before running this review.
review_regression_requirements: Check that delivered common code supports exactly the documented text protocol; Check that nickname validation, 1024-byte limit, and newline buffering are evidenced by tests or manual proof; Check that logger evidence covers startup, login, quit, abnormal disconnect, group chat, private chat, and errors; Check that non-goals such as auth, database, GUI, offline messages, and admin control are not introduced.
